title: Harary–Hill Conjecture
statement_text: The crossing number of the complete graph $K_n$ is $$H(n) = \frac{1}{4}\left\lfloor\frac{n}{2}\right\rfloor\left\lfloor\frac{n-1}{2}\right\rfloor\left\lfloor\frac{n-2}{2}\right\rfloor\left\lfloor\frac{n-3}{2}\right\rfloor.$$
context_text: The Harary–Hill conjecture, still open after more than 50 years, asserts that $\mathrm{cr}(K_n) = H(n)$. It is attributed to Anthony Hill and first published by Harary and Hill; it has been verified only for $n \leq 12$. The paper's central goal is to extend the family of drawings for which the conjecture is known to hold.
paper_contribution: Proves that every $\left(\lfloor n/2\rfloor - 2\right)$-bishellable drawing $D$ of $K_n$ satisfies $\mathrm{cr}(D) \geq H(n)$, strictly enlarging the class of drawings covered beyond the previously known shellable drawings (Theorem 4), and exhibits an infinite family of drawings that are bishellable but not shellable (Theorem 5).
notes: Floor-bracket symbols appear as (cid:22)/(cid:23) artifacts in the PDF extraction; the formula has been reconstructed from context and is unambiguous.
